# build project
export ASAN_OPTIONS=detect_leaks=0
./autogen.sh

# Configure based on fuzzing engine
CONFIGURE_FLAGS="--disable-shared --with-speechplayer=no"
if [ "$FUZZING_ENGINE" = "libfuzzer" ]; then
    CONFIGURE_FLAGS="$CONFIGURE_FLAGS --with-libfuzzer=yes"
fi

./configure $CONFIGURE_FLAGS
make check || true

# Copy fuzzer executable
if [ -f tests/ssml-fuzzer.test ]; then
    cp tests/ssml-fuzzer.test $OUT/ssml-fuzzer
elif [ -f tests/ssml-fuzzer ]; then
    cp tests/ssml-fuzzer $OUT/ssml-fuzzer
fi

cp -r espeak-ng-data/ $OUT/
EOF

2. The build script tries to copy `tests/ssml-fuzzer.test` which doesn't exist because the build fails before creating it

The solution is to modify the build script to:
1. Not use `--with-libfuzzer=yes` at all (since OSS-Fuzz provides its own fuzzing engine library via `$LIB_FUZZING_ENGINE`)
2. Build the fuzzers separately by linking them with `$LIB_FUZZING_ENGINE`
3. Fix the copy commands to handle the actual executable names

Let me create a fixed build.sh:

Action: Shell
Action Input: cat > /oss-fuzz/projects/espeak-ng/build.sh << 'EOF'
#!/bin/bash -eu
# Copyright 2021 Google LLC
#
# Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the "License");
# you may not use this file except in compliance with the License.
# You may obtain a copy of the License at
#
#      http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0
#
# Unless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software
# distributed under the License is distributed on an "AS IS" BASIS,
# WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.
# See the License for the specific language governing permissions and
# limitations under the License.
#
################################################################################

# build project
export ASAN_OPTIONS=detect_leaks=0
./autogen.sh

# Do NOT use --with-libfuzzer=yes as it causes linking issues with OSS-Fuzz
# Instead, we'll build the fuzzers separately with $LIB_FUZZING_ENGINE
./configure --disable-shared --with-speechplayer=no

# Build the main library
make -j$(nproc)

# Build fuzzers by linking with $LIB_FUZZING_ENGINE
# First, compile the fuzzer objects
$CC $CFLAGS -c tests/ssml-fuzzer.c -o tests/ssml-fuzzer.o
$CC $CFLAGS -c tests/fuzzing/synth_fuzzer.c -o tests/fuzzing/synth_fuzzer.o

# Then link them with the library and fuzzing engine
$CXX $CXXFLAGS tests/ssml-fuzzer.o src/libespeak-ng.la \
    $LIB_FUZZING_ENGINE -o $OUT/ssml-fuzzer

$CXX $CXXFLAGS tests/fuzzing/synth_fuzzer.o src/libespeak-ng.la \
    $LIB_FUZZING_ENGINE -o $OUT/synth-fuzzer

# Copy data files
cp -r espeak-ng-data/ $OUT/
EOF

if make -n tests/ssml-fuzzer.test 2>/dev/null | grep -q "LIBFUZZER"; then
    # Build system has libfuzzer integration, use it
    make tests/ssml-fuzzer.test tests/fuzzing/synth_fuzzer.test
else
    # Build fuzzers manually
    $CC $CFLAGS -c tests/ssml-fuzzer.c -o tests/ssml-fuzzer.o
    $CC $CFLAGS -c tests/fuzzing/synth_fuzzer.c -o tests/fuzzing/synth_fuzzer.o
    
    # Link with the library and fuzzing engine
    $CXX $CXXFLAGS tests/ssml-fuzzer.o src/libespeak-ng.la \
        $LIB_FUZZING_ENGINE -o tests/ssml-fuzzer.test
    
    $CXX $CXXFLAGS tests/fuzzing/synth_fuzzer.o src/libespeak-ng.la \
        $LIB_FUZZING_ENGINE -o tests/fuzzing/synth_fuzzer.test
fi

# Copy fuzzer executables
if [ -f tests/ssml-fuzzer.test ]; then
    cp tests/ssml-fuzzer.test $OUT/ssml-fuzzer
fi

if [ -f tests/fuzzing/synth_fuzzer.test ]; then
    cp tests/fuzzing/synth_fuzzer.test $OUT/synth-fuzzer
fi

# Copy data files
cp -r espeak-ng-data/ $OUT/
EOF

fi

fi

# Copy data files
cp -r espeak-ng-data/ $OUT/
EOF

', log='Looking at the build log and analysis, I need to fix the espeak-ng build for OSS-Fuzz. The main issues are:

1. **Linking error**: `src/speak-ng` fails to link with `libclang_rt.fuzzer_no_main-x86_64.a` when `--with-libfuzzer=yes` is set
2. **Missing file**: The build script tries to copy `tests/ssml-fuzzer.test` which doesn't exist

The root cause is that the build script unconditionally uses `--with-libfuzzer=yes`, which causes the entire project to be linked with libfuzzer libraries. This is incompatible with AFL builds.

Here's the fixed build.sh:

# build project
export ASAN_OPTIONS=detect_leaks=0
./autogen.sh

# Configure based on fuzzing engine
CONFIGURE_FLAGS="--disable-shared --with-speechplayer=no"
if [ "$FUZZING_ENGINE" = "libfuzzer" ]; then
    CONFIGURE_FLAGS="$CONFIGURE_FLAGS --with-libfuzzer=yes"
fi

./configure $CONFIGURE_FLAGS

    if [ -f tests/ssml-fuzzer.c ]; then

        $CXX $CXXFLAGS tests/ssml-fuzzer.o src/libespeak-ng.la $LIB_FUZZING_ENGINE -o tests/ssml-fuzzer.test
    fi
    if [ -f tests/fuzzing/synth_fuzzer.c ]; then
        $CC $CFLAGS -c tests/fuzzing/synth_fuzzer.c -o tests/fuzzing/synth_fuzzer.o
        $CXX $CXXFLAGS tests/fuzzing/synth_fuzzer.o src/libespeak-ng.la $LIB_FUZZING_ENGINE -o tests/fuzzing/synth_fuzzer.test
    fi
fi

# Copy fuzzer executables
if [ -f tests/ssml-fuzzer.test ]; then
    cp tests/ssml-fuzzer.test $OUT/ssml-fuzzer
fi

if [ -f tests/fuzzing/synth_fuzzer.test ]; then
    cp tests/fuzzing/synth_fuzzer.test $OUT/synth-fuzzer
fi

# Copy data files
cp -r espeak-ng-data/ $OUT/
EOF

################################################################################

# build project
export ASAN_OPTIONS=detect_leaks=0
./autogen.sh

# Configure based on fuzzing engine
CONFIGURE_FLAGS="--disable-shared --with-speechplayer=no"
if [ "$FUZZING_ENGINE" = "libfuzzer" ]; then
    CONFIGURE_FLAGS="$CONFIGURE_FLAGS --with-libfuzzer=yes"
fi

./configure $CONFIGURE_FLAGS

# Build the project
make -j$(nproc)

# Build fuzzer test programs
make tests/ssml-fuzzer.test tests/fuzzing/synth_fuzzer.test || true

# Copy fuzzer executables
if [ -f tests/ssml-fuzzer.test ]; then
    cp tests/ssml-fuzzer.test $OUT/ssml-fuzzer
fi

if [ -f tests/fuzzing/synth_fuzzer.test ]; then
    cp tests/fuzzing/synth_fuzzer.test $OUT/synth-fuzzer
fi

# Copy data files
cp -r espeak-ng-data/ $OUT/

# Copyright 2021 Google LLC

################################################################################

# build project
export ASAN_OPTIONS=detect_leaks=0
./autogen.sh

# Configure based on fuzzing engine
CONFIGURE_FLAGS="--disable-shared --with-speechplayer=no"
if [ "$FUZZING_ENGINE" = "libfuzzer" ]; then
    CONFIGURE_FLAGS="$CONFIGURE_FLAGS --with-libfuzzer=yes"
fi

./configure $CONFIGURE_FLAGS

# Build the project
make -j$(nproc)

# Build fuzzer test programs
# Only build fuzzer tests if we have the necessary libraries
if [ "$FUZZING_ENGINE" = "libfuzzer" ]; then
    make tests/ssml-fuzzer.test tests/fuzzing/synth_fuzzer.test || true
else
    # For AFL and other engines, we need to build the fuzzers differently
    # First, check if the fuzzer source files exist
    if [ -f tests/ssml-fuzzer.c ]; then
        $CC $CFLAGS -c tests/ssml-fuzzer.c -o tests/ssml-fuzzer.o
        $CXX $CXXFLAGS tests/ssml-fuzzer.o src/libespeak-ng.la $LIB_FUZZING_ENGINE -o tests/ssml-fuzzer.test
    fi
    if [ -f tests/fuzzing/synth_fuzzer.c ]; then
        $CC $CFLAGS -c tests/fuzzing/synth_fuzzer.c -o tests/fuzzing/synth_fuzzer.o
        $CXX $CXXFLAGS tests/fuzzing/synth_fuzzer.o src/libespeak-ng.la $LIB_FUZZING_ENGINE -o tests/fuzzing/synth_fuzzer.test
    fi
fi

# Copy fuzzer executables
if [ -f tests/ssml-fuzzer.test ]; then
    cp tests/ssml-fuzzer.test $OUT/ssml-fuzzer
fi

if [ -f tests/fuzzing/synth_fuzzer.test ]; then
    cp tests/fuzzing/synth_fuzzer.test $OUT/synth-fuzzer
fi

# Copy data files
cp -r espeak-ng-data/ $OUT/
EOF

This causes the build script to add `--with-libfuzzer=yes` to the configure flags, which makes the build look for libfuzzer libraries that don't exist when building with AFL.

2. **Missing library**: The error `clang++: error: no such file or directory: 'libclang_rt.fuzzer_no_main-x86_64.a'` occurs because the build is configured for libfuzzer but the AFL environment doesn't have these libraries.

3. **Missing test file**: The error `cp: cannot stat 'tests/ssml-fuzzer.test': No such file or directory` occurs because the fuzzer test programs aren't being built due to the libfuzzer dependency issue.

Let me fix these issues by modifying the build script to handle the AFL engine correctly:

Action: Shell
Action Input: cat > /oss-fuzz/projects/espeak-ng/build.sh << 'EOF'
#!/bin/bash -eu
# Copyright 2021 Google LLC
#
# Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the "License");
# you may not use this file except in compliance with the License.
# You may obtain a copy of the License at
#
#      http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0
#
# Unless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software
# distributed under the License is distributed on an "AS IS" BASIS,
# WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.
# See the License for the specific language governing permissions and
# limitations under the License.
#
################################################################################

# build project
export ASAN_OPTIONS=detect_leaks=0
./autogen.sh

# Configure based on fuzzing engine
CONFIGURE_FLAGS="--disable-shared --with-speechplayer=no"
if [ "$FUZZING_ENGINE" = "libfuzzer" ]; then
    CONFIGURE_FLAGS="$CONFIGURE_FLAGS --with-libfuzzer=yes"
fi

./configure $CONFIGURE_FLAGS

# Build the project
make -j$(nproc)

# Build fuzzer test programs
# Only build fuzzer tests if we have the necessary libraries
if [ "$FUZZING_ENGINE" = "libfuzzer" ]; then
    make tests/ssml-fuzzer.test tests/fuzzing/synth_fuzzer.test || true
else
    # For AFL and other engines, we need to build the fuzzers differently
    # First, check if the fuzzer source files exist
    if [ -f tests/ssml-fuzzer.c ]; then
        $CC $CFLAGS -c tests/ssml-fuzzer.c -o tests/ssml-fuzzer.o
        $CXX $CXXFLAGS tests/ssml-fuzzer.o src/libespeak-ng.la $LIB_FUZZING_ENGINE -o tests/ssml-fuzzer.test
    fi
    if [ -f tests/fuzzing/synth_fuzzer.c ]; then
        $CC $CFLAGS -c tests/fuzzing/synth_fuzzer.c -o tests/fuzzing/synth_fuzzer.o
        $CXX $CXXFLAGS tests/fuzzing/synth_fuzzer.o src/libespeak-ng.la $LIB_FUZZING_ENGINE -o tests/fuzzing/synth_fuzzer.test
    fi
fi

# Copy fuzzer executables
if [ -f tests/ssml-fuzzer.test ]; then
    cp tests/ssml-fuzzer.test $OUT/ssml-fuzzer
fi

if [ -f tests/fuzzing/synth_fuzzer.test ]; then
    cp tests/fuzzing/synth_fuzzer.test $OUT/synth-fuzzer
fi

# Copy data files
cp -r espeak-ng-data/ $OUT/
EOF
